title: Add hidden --internal-print-default-config CLI branch
action: Add a hidden, self-exec-only CLI branch --internal-print-default-config, mirroring the existing --internal-merge-config pattern (main.rs:29-32,86,168-171): a new Args.internal_print_default_config: bool field, parsed in parse_args, never documented in print_help(). When set, main() calls a new pub fn run_internal_print_default_config() -> i32 in src/config/mod.rs (use crate:: internally, never herdr_go::) that resolves root via default_config_root(&home()) (per D2, reusing the previous cell's helper), prints default_config_json(&root) to stdout via println!, and returns 0 -- then main exits via std::process::exit on that code, exactly like the internal_merge_config branch does. This is the mechanism D4 locks: install.sh/install.ps1 (later cells) will call this flag on the freshly-downloaded binary and capture stdout to obtain the canonical config.json content instead of hand-writing JSON. Add a unit test named exactly run_internal_print_default_config_matches_default_config_json in src/config/mod.rs's tests module, asserting run_internal_print_default_config's stdout (capture it directly, or refactor so the printed string is separately obtainable) equals default_config_json(&default_config_root(&some_test_home)) for a fixed test HOME -- verify greps for this exact function name, so name it precisely. The new Args field also means every existing test Args-struct literal must add internal_print_default_config: false (e.g. the literal around main.rs:469-478) or the crate fails to compile -- cargo build in verify catches this either way, but expect it.

truths: Running `herdr-go --internal-print-default-config` with a real HOME prints a JSON document byte-identical to what ensure_config would write for a fresh config.json under that HOME

artifacts.substantive: parses --internal-print-default-config and dispatches to run_internal_print_default_config, mirroring the internal_merge_config branch, exiting on its return code

key_links: main.rs's dispatch calls herdr_go::config::run_internal_print_default_config and process::exits on its return code

verify: grep -q 'internal_print_default_config' src/main.rs && grep -q 'fn run_internal_print_default_config' src/config/mod.rs && ! grep -q 'herdr_go::' src/config/mod.rs && grep -q 'fn run_internal_print_default_config_matches_default_config_json' src/config/mod.rs && cargo build --quiet && cargo test --quiet run_internal_print_default_config

trace.outcome: Added hidden --internal-print-default-config CLI branch: Args field + parse_args match arm in src/main.rs dispatches to new pub fn run_internal_print_default_config() -> i32 in src/config/mod.rs, which resolves root via default_config_root(&home()) and prints default_config_json(&root) verbatim to stdout via a testable default_config_for_current_home() helper. Flag omitted from print_help(). Unit test run_internal_print_default_config_matches_default_config_json added; existing Args test literal updated for the new field.
